```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
m <- sample(3:9,1); c0 <- 2/(m*(m+1))
vals <- c(c0,1/m,1/(m+1),m/(m+1),1/(m*(m+1)))
opts <- sprintf("$%.5f$",vals)
```

Question
========
Uma variável discreta tem $p_X(x)=c\,x$ para $x=1,\ldots,`r m`$. Qual valor normaliza a pmf?

Answerlist
----------
* `r opts[1]`
* `r opts[2]`
* `r opts[3]`
* `r opts[4]`
* `r opts[5]`

Solution
========
Deve valer
$$1=\sum_{x=1}^{`r m`}cx=c\frac{`r m`(`r m+1`)}2,$$
portanto $c=2/[`r m`(`r m+1`)]=`r sprintf("%.5f",c0)`$.

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap1S6Q1_normalizacao_pmf
extype: schoice
exsolution: 10000
exshuffle: TRUE
